methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $900M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Pacific Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Eric Lander and Will Regan that has raised $900M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo. This narrow peer set reflects the emerging stage of the frontier nuclear sector.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy cohort; only 1 same-stage peer and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified, falling below the 3-datapoint threshold for reliable triangulation. Accordingly, stage-progression methodology was the only viable approach, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement. Using the $900M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying this multiplier yields an implied valuation of $1.8B.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.8B. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x step-up multiplier for this capital-intensive sector—nuclear ventures may exhibit materially different progression profiles than broader venture cohorts—and the underlying validity of using total raised as a proxy for current valuation in an early-stage, pre-revenue frontier context. Refinement would benefit from access to later-stage round data or transaction multiples within the nuclear energy space.
